description_long: This is a double-headed battle axe featuring a robust, dark metallic blade with two symmetrical, crescent-shaped cutting edges. The blade is thick and has a pronounced central ridge, giving it a heavy-duty appearance suitable for chopping or combat. The handle is made of wood, slightly curved for ergonomic grip, and is reinforced with a greenish wrap around the midsection for added grip and comfort. The end of the handle is capped with a metal pommel, likely for balance and durability. The overall design is both functional and visually striking, with a blend of medieval and fantasy aesthetics. No visible brand or markings are present on the axe.
description: A double-headed, dark metal battle axe with a curved wooden handle and green grip wrap, featuring a robust, symmetrical blade design.
description_view_0: The axe is shown at an angle, highlighting the double-edged blade and the green grip wrap on the wooden handle.
description_view_1: The axe is viewed from the top, showing the narrow profile of the blade and the straight alignment of the handle.
description_view_2: The opposite angled view reveals the other side of the blade and the curvature of the handle, with the grip wrap visible.
description_view_3: The axe is seen from the bottom, displaying the thickness of the blade and the end cap of the handle.
attribute_handle_type: Curved wooden handle with a round cross-section, wrapped with a green grip for improved hold; ergonomic and slightly tapered.
attribute_blade_design: Double-headed, crescent-shaped steel blade with a thick central ridge and two symmetrical cutting edges; robust and functional.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting with the blade fitted onto the handle and secured, possibly with a metal collar.
